Issue: Replace direct np.random.* calls with np.random.RandomState instances for reproducibility
Decision: Replace direct np.random.* calls with np.random.RandomState instances in utility functions and signal transforms
Rationale: Improves reproducibility by avoiding global RNG state; utility functions respect np.random.seed() when rand_state is None; signal transforms use self.R for consistency
Author: haoyu-haoyu
Description: Utility functions in monai/transforms/utils.py, monai/data/synthetic.py, monai/data/utils.py, monai/utils/ordering.py and signal transforms in monai/transforms/signal/array.py
Cost: Low (reverting changes to utility functions; only signal transforms retained)
Risk: Low (reverting changes to utility functions avoids breaking reproducibility; signal transforms inconsistency fixed)

Argument.author: haoyu-haoyu
Argument.argument: Replacing global RNG with unseeded np.random.RandomState() breaks reproducibility for callers relying on np.random.seed(); utility functions should respect np.random.seed() when rand_state is None

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: Utility functions with optional rand_state should delegate to global RNG when None for deterministic behavior; signal/array.py fix is correct improvement
